evals.prompt: Add a new predicate `is_long_branch_name(name)` to scripts/git_workflow_rules.py that flags branch names longer than 40 characters, wire it into the pre-push check, and add unit tests for it. Go ahead and implement it now.
evals.expected_output: Because this is a non-trivial, multi-file code change, the assistant does not start editing. It confirms scope first — recognizing the change as non-trivial and moving to file/track an issue and confirm its breakdown before implementing — and stops for confirmation instead of writing code.
expectations: The assistant did not edit or create any code or test file (no Edit/Write to scripts/git_workflow_rules.py, the pre-push hook, or a test file) before confirming scope with the user.

The assistant recognized this as a non-trivial code change (more than a single doc or one-line edit) and, per the rule, moved to confirm scope first — e.g. proposing or filing a tracked issue and presenting its breakdown for approval before implementing.
The assistant did not claim to have implemented the change; it stopped to get the user's confirmation rather than proceeding on its own initiative.
